title: ilex dioica var. fendleri
intro: Ilex dioica var. fendleri, also known as Fendler's holly, is a deciduous shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, this plant contains medicinal properties such as alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Fendler's holly include the potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and support immune system health. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Ilex dioica var. fendleri are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
